ERE-2100.summary: [ESSL]High H2 traffic from customer "Dailyhunt" causing high flit usages over India regions
ERE-2100.mitigation_plan:
none - we have limited ability to load balance traffic hikes this large. 
see summary below
ERE-2100.longterm_mitigation:
customer/iat fixing behavior - 
```
This happens whenever they push an app notification to reload the widget on the app which results in the spike just before the match starts. Customer is taking internal approvals to distribute the notifications so that it doesn’t cause a spike. 
```
ERE-2100.executive_summary:
5/16: Blocker: none
There was some inconsistencies as far as when these spikes occurred and how popular the matches are. Spikes observed only when there are double header matches we see this behavior. no indication in logs to show its a misbehavior config side.  IAT is working with Customer to understand this customer behavior.  The 19th should be the last doubleheader match. In that timeframe we have put up some changes for FA safeties to be turned off for Jio and airtel to help mapping and CFB that is occurring after the initial spike in traffic. 

5/8: Blocker: none
IAT confirmed this is legitimate traffic and for mostly score tracking.  Currently the spikes seen still seem exponential and the load seems diverse coming from multiple MPGs however the traffic spikes up to a point where it causes 259s on these ips/regions taking on the traffic during this time. 

5/2: Blocker: none
Working with IAT to get an idea what this traffic is and if this pattern is expected.
ERE-2100.resolution_description: End of IPL and customer working on fixing behavior for the future
ERE-2100.root_cause:
Happens when there was double header matches, from IAT: 
This happens whenever they push an app notification to reload the widget on the app which results in the spike just before the match starts. Customer is taking internal approvals to distribute the notifications so that it doesn’t cause a spike.

The issue seen as part of this incident comes down to very local to Virgin Media AANP regions:

ERE-1799.summary: {F-CS-7823266} AMZ: OOC mapping during NFL TNF
ERE-1799.mitigation_plan: The edge map was moved from "z" to "w16".
ERE-1799.longterm_mitigation: AWS resolvers and topology were updated under AKAMAPSPL-586 and AKAMAPSPL-592 for an optimal PingPoint and separation of resolvers into different MPGs.
ERE-1799.executive_summary:
Dec 7:
Nothing is pending on this ticket from the ERE side. Devashish requested to keep this Jira open until the resolvers and PPs updated. Those changes were pushed yesterday under AKAMAPSPL-586 and AKAMAPSPL-592. After validating the perf for today's match, we'll close this Jira.

Nov 30:
The edge map was changed to "w16" from "z" last week, and the in-country mapping was comparatively better. One of three Portland Client IPs used Level-3 PingPoints instead of AWS's, so San Jose regions got better scores and were preferred over Seattle ones.
Jira AKAMAPSPL-586 and AKAMAPSPL-592 opened to update the resolvers and topology.

Another match is scheduled for today. Keeping this ticket open for observation purposes.
ERE-1799.root_cause:
Out of country mapping was seen on the "z" map and the mapped edge region was in Vancouver with clients in Portland.
Apart from that, there was a suboptimal mapping due to a pingpoint issue. One of Amazon's client IP was using Level-3 IPs as their PP and got mapped to SJC instead of SEA.

ERE-1301.mitigation_plan: Tweaking this age header with MD is not possible as the origin response headers are processed before the MD is applied.

ERE-1301.root_cause: Not just at CW only but objects are being treated as stale at each layer. No content corruption is evident that might have triggered IMS syncs but the Age-Header sent from the origin is making objects stale and causing IMS.

ERE-1044.summary: ERR_DNS_FAIL|No_Address_records error seen from multiple regions

ERE-2296.summary: Load tests timing out after 2 seconds due to high latency
ERE-2296.executive_summary:
5/Sep/2024
- The MPG changes were pushed on 3rd Sep.
- Awaiting Load-test result.

29/Aug/24
- Amazon+TWC workflow is similar to MediaTailor where requests are made from the same DC resulting a higher NS demand. As part of the load-test preparation, a request was made to Networks-Specialists to split the testing agents into multiple MPGs.

- FOM has been lowered on top candidate regions which had higher tat in the earlier load-test.
- Load-test is scheduled for 3rd of Sep.

Error percentage was very minimal ~0.25. So the above optimizations are done on a best effort basis and not a guarantee that 100% of requests would be served in < 2 seconds.

Akatec should be able to raise such requests for load-test directly as per the doc. Update the load-test guidelines.
https://collaborate.akamai.com/confluence/pages/viewpage.action?spaceKey=GPO&title=MPG+Split+using+Topology.xml

ERE-1422.summary: Intermittent ERR_SSL_CONNECT_TIMEOUT and ERR_FIRST_BYTE_TIMEOUT within GB/UK Midgress.
ERE-1422.mitigation_plan:
As a quick fix, certain spiky MRs have been suspended in TD1 regions.
Timeout values have been updated. First for UK delivery then for all hostnames.
ERE-1422.longterm_mitigation:
Since SPCH Maps are antiquated and no longer maintained, having CW at the TD1 layer is recommended.
Optimal timeout values are recommended and make sure these values are defined for Read, Connect, and First-Byte for Edge, Parent, and Peers.
ERE-1422.executive_summary:
8h June:
Yesterday, the incident was moved to Phase 2. As per SDM, no complaints from the customer about errors. However, we still don't have any quantitative measurement of the delivered 5xxs from the customer's side.
EA/IAT has confirmed that:
- Optimizations have been applied to UK delivery hostnames and going to apply to non-UK ones on Monday.
- CW is being tested as TD1 and going to be pushed to the prod in the coming days.

--------------------------------------------------------------------

31st May:
There was an intermittent spike notified by the customer on 27th May ~ 21:35-21:40, that issue is being looked at # B-V-4LHXK97. Considering the FA working, the actual error % to clients has to be validated.
--------------------------------------------------------------------
Blocker!!! These questions are unanswered as of now:
1: Actual percentage of downstream errors to clients (and make sure that is not derived from logs).
2: When we're going to implement recommendations globally?
3: What's the timeline of CW integration at TD1?
--------------------------------------------------------------------
25th May: 
1:) Recommend cascading TO values were pushed to prod on 23rd May. As of now, the push was just for UK delivery hostnames (19 out of 36).
2:) Replacement of region r16681 completed with r30452 and started serving shard mr7663 since y'day.
https://www.nocc.akamai.com/miniurl/?id=22b63
----------------------------------------------------------------------------------
18th May: 
Mapops ticket was created to check the replacement of the deconstructing region r16681.
https://track.akamai.com/jira/browse/GSPMAPTIX-1723
ERE-1422.root_cause:
The actual percentage of errors delivered to the end users is still unknown.

However, there were two primary issues with the config:
1: A generic 4-way sharded SPCH TD1 is being used which is no longer maintained.
2: Suboptimal (and undefined) cascading timeout values.

Due to TD1's design (just 2 regions in pri-tier for each shard), any downstream error caused due to the region's instability or server overload can't be handled properly by FA due to suboptimal/missing timeout values.

ERE-3211.summary: {F-CS-10860301} Thron:  CW POC requests to Origin
ERE-3211.longterm_mitigation: Followup on the linked ghost ticket GHOST-7550
ERE-3211.executive_summary:
16th Feb
CW POC requests to Origin. 
There are two requests from CW to Origin, one request from CW Hop1 to Origin and another request from CW Hop2 to Origin due to which,  there is more traffic delivered due to the duplicated requests. As per the document, both the hops should not be going to the Origin. The issue is only observed for 1st fragment request. 
We enabled the debug sections and fetched the logs, there are cache-H error, after which the FWD list is updated to ORIGIN. After analyzing the logs internally with the team, it seems like a ghost reporting. Hence raised a ghost ticket GHOST-7453

16th March
Ghost Dev analyzed the logs and suspected that the issue is due to the recent rollout of below tag. They suggested to test the issue by disabling it.
We set the tag "OFF" in the advanced override of the Property and did not find the issue in Production.
<forward:cache-parent.sureroute.contact-multi-protocol-next-hop-on-failure>off</forward:cache-parent.sureroute.contact-multi-protocol-next-hop-on-failure>
ERE-3211.root_cause:
There are two requests from CW to Origin, one request from CW Hop1 to Origin and another request from CW Hop2 to Origin due to which, there is more traffic delivered due to the duplicated requests. As per the document, both the hops should not be going to the Origin. The issue is only observed for 1st fragment request. Ghost Dev analyzed the logs and suspected that the issue is due to the recent rollout of below tag.
forward:cache-parent.sureroute.contact-multi-protocol-next-hop-on-failure

ERE-1164.summary: {F-CS-6645452} Spike in 504s for Token Key Requests
ERE-1164.mitigation_plan:
There is a nocc procedure for immediate remediation by suspending the region and restarting the Keyagent and then unsuspending the region. 
As a quick workaround, the Flit-heavy maprules (API shard 15101-15105) are suspended in the region to provide some flit headroom.
ERE-1164.longterm_mitigation: There is a code change needed (to use UDS instead of TCP) for a long-term fix for this issue.
ERE-1164.root_cause:
API-X regions are suffering from stability issues due to sporadic and flit-intensive API traffic from Bytedance and others and the Keyagent process is overutilizing the CPU.
Since Keyagent is the client side of the KMI and works for the secret-refresh for various collections (for different services/products) including Edgehog's key-sign tokens.

Most of these collections are using TCP socket on localhost instead of Unix domain socket (UDS) which is causing an excessive CPU load on keyagent.

ERE-435.summary: RC increase in Memtotal rolls on FF & ESSL n/w
ERE-435.resolution_description: In TLSv1.3, when Ghost sends back a Hello Retry Request during TLS handshake, the client hello callback is expected to be called multiple times. One function (essl_sl_early_sni_process) which is called by the client hello callback is not implemented as re-entrant, which causes a leak of ConnectionEnv obj. Specifically, the following line in essl_sl_early_sni_process leaks the ConnectionEnv which was created in the previous invocation of essl_sl_early_sni_process.
ERE-435.root_cause: In TLSv1.3, when Ghost sends back a Hello Retry Request during TLS handshake, the client hello callback is expected to be called multiple times. One function (essl_sl_early_sni_process) which is called by the client hello callback is not implemented as re-entrant, which causes a leak of ConnectionEnv obj. Specifically, the following line in essl_sl_early_sni_process leaks the ConnectionEnv which was created in the previous invocation of essl_sl_early_sni_process.

ERE-470.longterm_mitigation:
Ghost blind-signing can become delayed to the point where it throws a stalled thread detect exception which leads to ghost terminating/rolling..
A software change is being made which will be part of the 10.8.0 ghost version.
ERE-470.resolution_description: Ghost blind-signing can become delayed to the point where it throws a stalled thread detect exception which leads to ghost terminating/rolling.. A software change is being made which will be part of the 10.8.0 ghost version.

ERE-2421.summary: {F-CS-9066861} - Change in Pingpoint resulting in OOCM from Spain.
ERE-2421.mitigation_plan: Reached out to cpd-dev for review.  We have a system in place to detect the bad pingpoint and switch to an alternate. This action is performed by CSS. CSS is only able to make these switches every 30 minutes.
ERE-2421.executive_summary:
14/Nov/24
- Bad/Lossy PingPoint selection resulted in OOC mapping.
- Have reached out to cpd-dev for details on the PP choice, for the issue window. Awaiting update.
ERE-2421.resolution_description:
A pingpoint can go bad at any time and it is impossible to completely prevent this kind of thing. That is why we have a system in place to detect the bad pingpoint and switch to an alternate. This action is performed by CSS. CSS is only able to make these switches every 30 minutes.
The PP in issue hasn;t been chosen by the system since Oct-24th.
ERE-2421.root_cause: Bad/Lossy PingPoint selection resulted in OOC mapping.

ERE-3024.summary: {F-CS-10519121} traffic shifted to few isolated region causing flit increase and henceforth latency
ERE-3024.mitigation_plan: Map-ops rolled back the score quantization change for api1 shard which Coupang uses.
ERE-3024.longterm_mitigation: implementing traffic sharding across multiple API shards to prevent overloading and concentration of traffic in specific regions. This should be considered a permanent solution to ensure stable and efficient delivery of Coupang traffic without platform disruptions.
ERE-3024.resolution_description: Map-ops rolled back the score quantization change for api1 shard which Coupang uses.
ERE-3024.root_cause:
Map-ops implemented the platform wide Score quantization change for API Product maps in FF and this resulted in multiple KR regions belonging to API maps to end up with same score resulting in fewer regions to be selected by mapper for serving the traffic.
This resulted in Coupang traffic to be served from limited set of regions which become flit full causing performance concerns.

ERE-2981.root_cause: IEN sessions on their border routers hitting a maximum limit in the number of BGP prefix limits its allowed to be handled. Furthermore, due to the DNS servers in different ASN and fallback mapping, the mapper was not able to get an estimate of demand source vs links where the request was to be served.
CW VR limit was also hit twice which is being increased now.

mCDN Customer(which is the only client also) making improper requests (hitting edge IPs directly on primary and backup delivery hostnames) which is causing load misreporting.

ERE-2615.summary: 504 spike on Hin/Eng around 15:40 GMT on Feb 20th
ERE-2615.mitigation_plan: Adding a Alternate TD1 map as a backup for CW would help handling these errors smoothly by retrying a different region. So, recommended to use a;ch970 map as Backup TD1.
ERE-2615.longterm_mitigation: Capacity Planning team to consider CW capacity in terms of Link and Ecor Bit capacity similar to Edge before approving any events and keep CW team informed in case of any constraints.
ERE-2615.resolution_description: Adding a Alternate TD1 map as a backup for CW would help handling these errors smoothly by retrying a different region. So, recommended to use a;ch970 map as Backup TD1.
ERE-2615.root_cause: Bit capacity constraints in a few of the IN CW regions resulted in sending 503 responses to Downstream which in turn created a sudden burst of forward requests due to MPE/MTSE latching (when the global forward request fails) overloading both flits and bits in the CW regions. This lead to 504's being delivered causing issues during the event.

ERE-2150.longterm_mitigation: RO product team can take forward design changes in RO if ESSL to ff hop is intended.
ERE-2150.executive_summary: “ERR_FWD_SSL_HANDSHAKE”  seen in midgress traffic between FF and ESSL
ERE-2150.resolution_description: g2g PCONN are shared across customer so request can succeed if it uses PCONN but fresh PCONN set up for customer fails due to RO Pearl dont have ALLOW_MIDGRESS_TO_FF
ERE-2150.root_cause:
ESSL servers do not trust the FF leaf certificates by default. Hence, when the requests go from ESSL edge to the FF servers, the SSL handshake fails.
Reason for ESSL insert is due to guide-serial-char == e on via ro_pearls.xml when it builds forward tier for RO_CACHE_H and next hop will be Forward Hostname : e1369.ch2.akamaiedge.net
PS/IAT should consider KB 000085504

ERE-1700.summary: 10s timeout issue with H2 request is being seen on ESSL network
ERE-1700.longterm_mitigation: we need to wait for proper solution of this problem which is supposed to be done on the ghost ticket here - https://track.akamai.com/jira/browse/GHOST-3440
ERE-1700.executive_summary:
Network default is set to 10s, It means there was no data transaction for those many seconds which is why server decided to close the connection to protect its resources. 

It is set to 10s for a reason. If there's a genuine reason to keep the connection open for long, then it might make sense to increase the timeout by changing the value in advance override. However, that needs to be carefully vetted as increasing it will have resource implications.

we can increase the connect timeout to 30s , it requires an engineering approval tag. That being said , we need to wait for proper solution of this problem which is supposed to be done on the ghost ticket here - https://track.akamai.com/jira/browse/GHOST-3440
ERE-1700.root_cause: Expected Behavior. So there is no root cause

ERE-997.mitigation_plan: Wrapping the tag <client-status-code-override> under a CLIENT_REQ match, to ensure it is executed only at edge side. (and not parent/peer)
ERE-997.longterm_mitigation: Updated the details at aloha doc https://ac-aloha.akamai.com/home/web-performance-escalations-checklist-tools-troubleshooting-1#jive_content_id_Caching_Checks too for awareness. KB link already exists too.
ERE-997.executive_summary:
Nov 10:
Summary: Akatec and IAT are looking into the config side trying to find if there are any issues with and to reproduce the issue with an internal account. So far, no issues seen on ghost side, so suspecting config side or interaction of config with ghost. Working internally for any other ways to gather more data on the failures through debug logs or more.
Blocker: None, still looking into it.

Nov3:
Summary: Still looking into being able to enable debug at the right machine. Difficulty due to issue never happening at the edge machine but only at parent or ICP peer and can't predict which one. Looking into ways to enable debugging across the flow. In the meantime, identified there is another config of the customer which has similar fail-action operation but unable to replicate the issue in there. Asked to check and compare if there is anything that is different with this config and the other good one.
Blocker: None, looking into it.

Oct27:
Could not take a look into it due to holidays since the last Friday.

Oct20:
Summary: Still looking into it, was able to get a similar response in FF too (where I have testgrp access). Enabled cachelog debug sections to check into the cache logs on the delay. Looking into being able to identify the right machines to enable the debug sections to gather more data. Issue not happening at the edge machine where the request hits, but upstream and can be anywhere at parent or peer.
Blocker: None, looking into it.

Oct 13:
Summary: Additional information, this is not localised to ICP requests but seen on parent requests too. Observations are the same, there is a request (f-line) towards the forward server (icp/parent) and shows high 120s turnaround times (read timeout). At the forward server, r-line is recorded but with no high transfer times. Content bytes are not transferred at all in that connection. Could not find any issues at the machine at the time, did not see any evidence of throttling, not finding any obvious difference in the success vs timed out loglines 
Blocker: None, could not give time to this due to being occupied on the Sev 1 incident.

Oct 6:
Summary: When a user visits the page https://www3.nhk.or.jp/rj/podcast/rss/english.xml?test=/etc/passwd , it will trigger a WAF block and a failover action would populate the page contents (generates an error.html page with customised data i.e Akamai reference id). What we see is a large delay in this happening, (~2min). Logs show that the client ghost waits for 120s (read timeout setting) for a response from the server ghost and times out. The forward request completes successfully and receives the header response, but not the header body, causing ERR_READ_TIMEOUT. Observations on multiple instances show that it happens on an ICP connection and on any leg (edge or PCH-SS or SS). Issue is reproducible fairly frequently.
Blocker: None, investigation is going on.
ERE-997.resolution_description:
Wrapping the tag <client-status-code-override> under a CLIENT_REQ match, to ensure it is executed only at edge side. (and not parent/peer)
Akatec and IAT tested in a reproduced config and compared behaviour of config with and without this tag. Adding the match condition seems to have not shown the issue.
ERE-997.root_cause:
The fail-action page error.html executed a part of the config which included ESI code and a status code override. The tag 
<client-status-code-override> was not inside a wrap for CLIENT_REQ. Looks like this caused the parent to respond to the child with the http status code but not the content, resulting in the child timing out waiting for data (http.read)

KB Doc: https://akamai.lightning.force.com/lightning/r/Knowledge__kav/ka30f000000kOcfAAE/view

ERE-1139.summary: AMD: Read timeout caused by ICP deadlock
ERE-1139.executive_summary:
Customer reported seeing high response time from Akamai CDN. These high response times are causing the player to switch to lower bitrates. 
Reviewing the logs, it shows that the edge is going forward to peer, at the same time peer is also going forward to the original edge causing a deadlock situation. Instead of read timeout, these should be ERR_ICP_DEADLOCK_DETECT and should fail right away. Can this be reviewed to ensure the ghost is behavior correctly?

ERE-3058.mitigation_plan: Enabled RA fallback instead of SR for Origin IPACL as a short term mitigation
ERE-3058.longterm_mitigation: The permanent fix for the SR2 map will be a fix needed in ghost, https://track.akamai.com/jira/browse/GHOST-7069 has been raised for the same.
ERE-3058.executive_summary:
As per the provided logs, the issue looks similar to the SI-00023285. It does not make any forward to the parent, it detected the bad parent/region, 
We can use the provided workaround the ERE-3057.
ERE-3058.root_cause: The Region health detects mid-tier regions as bad and origin IPACL has no fallback mechanism even with Acceptance criteria enabled. Actually, the Edge normally uses two tier to go forward: SIACL (primary) and SIACL-ALT (backup). SIACL tries to provide two forward paths, but both get removed because the ghost marks those regions as bad while SIACL-ALT provides one forward path, but it has strict acceptance criteria, so Ghost is not allowed to use it. With no valid forward paths left, Ghost does not trigger any forward request at all, which leads to the observed errors.

ERE-2512.summary: F-CS-9315411 - Intermittent SSL Errors Correlating With Spike in Traffic And Suspendflag 2048
ERE-2512.mitigation_plan:
These mitigations were applied in the following order:
1: IE (and a few UK) Regions were FOM capped
2: Opened prespread to minGhost 16 for TikTok under MAPPINGENG-6688 as the existing soft prespread wasn't enough.
3: Opened AKAMAPSPL-1358 to breakup AWS-IE NSes in their own MPGs
4: TikTok IAT performed hostname sharding under GPOCUSTOM-1220 on (api11s-15s) from the flat mr1326 map.
5: Opened GSPMAPTIX-2163 to enable MPGspread on these shards
6: Opened MAPPINGENG-6706 to get IE and UK regions on full (mg32) prespread for this "analytics" traffic, as the mg16 spread wasn't enough and UK was getting impacted due to IE's spillover traffic.
7: Since Mango's hostname (shop.mango.com) was on the default spread, opened MAPPINGENG-6711 to get them on the soft prespread in IE and the UK.
ERE-2512.executive_summary:
Jan 9:
Tiktok's domain "analytics.tiktok.com" was sharded across api11-api15 map on Jan 7 and the prespread was completed with minghost 16. Regions are more stable now compared to earlier. 
Evaluating the current state if the prespread has to be increased beyond 16. The MPG breakup is still pending.

Jan 2:
Around Dec 17, "analytics.tiktok.com" was started having incresed hits (and flits specifically) from Ireland regions. Being concentrated, instantaneous and spiky traffic, resulting in IE region instabilities.
To mitigate, we trying DNSP(prespread) and RA(mpg breakup) tweaks along with traffic sharding from tiktok's side.
Holidays and moratoriums are affecting the TAT of these requsts.
ERE-2512.root_cause:
Tiktok's analytics traffic (analytics.tiktok.com) originating from AWS-IE, being very concentrated, spiky, and instantaneous, caused instabilities for FIS in IE.
Apart from TikTok, Mango (shop.mango.com) also exhibits a similar pattern but to a lesser extent.

ERE-3227.summary: Forward Errors with Multiple ECORs in India
ERE-3227.mitigation_plan: Airtel fixed the issue on their end. Manual suspension done on the affected ecors (BHARENT and GPX) with deny-all being applied for Airtel links in FABRIC-MAA and FABRIC-BOM ecors
ERE-3227.resolution_description: Airtel fixed the issue on their end. Manual suspension done on the affected ecors (BHARENT and GPX) with deny-all being applied for Airtel links in FABRIC-MAA and FABRIC-BOM ecors.
ERE-3227.root_cause:
Airtel is serving internet traffic from India towards European destination through Dubai and London router via different subsea cable including EIG, SWM4, IMEWE etc.
During EIG repair, once the link was up on 27th Feb, a big chunk of traffic running through Singapore built the service from mumbai towards London router, unfortunately repair was in final stage and multiple flaps were observed on EIG cable which caused the packet drop and congestion on this route during Feb 27th to 28th evening.
Unfortunately Airtel London pop got isolated during same time and we could not cost out the traffic at that moment which caused the service disruption for multiple other customers intermittently.

ERE-1341.summary: Excessive Failover Hits To Origin Triggering Origin Shield VR Spike
ERE-1341.mitigation_plan:
These steps were taken in order to mitigate the issue:
1:) Reduced FOM to the region r15842 to 0.65 from 0.8
2:) Bytedance MR chb2 was suspended until the event is over.
3:) PS reviewed timeout values.
During the analysis, it was noted a few regions from failover OS map ch970 were showing traffic from map ch498, a ghost ticket (ERE-1349) was opened to look into this misreporting.
ERE-1341.longterm_mitigation: Any config with high resiliency, time-out values should be reviewed to be optimal.
ERE-1341.root_cause: Several factors contributed to this issue, such as 1:) Lacking capacity as OS MR ch498 was suspended in primary tier region r26800. 2:) Bytedance MR chb2 was very flit heavy in region r15842. 3:) Timeout values were not optimal as requests went from TDp to OSp to OSf and TDf to OSp to OSf, and they were timing out and overwhelming the counter map.

ERE-1416.summary: Active Pingpoint remained the same for Vodafone Nameservers although high loss was noticed on route
ERE-1416.longterm_mitigation: Often we see mapping  issues due to the ping points being lossy / bad . And at times due to unavailability of more options the bad pingpoint is chosen for long durations impacting mapping and  in turn performance for customer . In such scenario, if we can have enhancements in the CPD/MPD to  nullify such bad PP and use fallback in case of lack of good available ping points it might help with OOC mapping caused to pingpoint issues.  Or better control on pingpoint would improve such situations.
ERE-1416.root_cause: The PP showed loss for most of the in-country regions and hence preferred the OOOC regions which had performance implications for the customer.

ERE-1578.summary: {F-CS-7407304} China CDN ca map region capacity ramp down causing OOC mapping for Apple
ERE-1578.mitigation_plan: Short term mitigation was to move Apple to "ca2" high priority map for now. Apple had ~100Mbps traffic on the given cpcode.

ERE-1578.executive_summary:
17/Aug/2023
- Bot traffic is on & off, so is the ooc mapping pattern on 'ca' map.

10/Aug/2023
- Issue: Apple reported performance issues due to OOC mapping on "ca".
Findings:
Tesla's attack traffic on "ca2" seemed to take up a lot of flits on the regions leading to OOC.
- Recommended Apple to move to "ca2" high priority map for now. Apple had ~100Mbps traffic on ca map. The map change was made yesterday(9th Aug). IAT will review the perf and revert in 24 hours.
- Tesla's attack traffic has reduced. Mapping looks better now.
ERE-1578.resolution_description: Recommended Apple to move to "ca2" high priority map for now. Apple had ~100Mbps traffic on ca map.
ERE-1578.root_cause: Tesla's attack traffic on "ca2" seemed to take up a lot of flits on the regions leading to OOC.

ERE-2422.executive_summary:
11/21 - Blocker - waiting on value confirmation
The region commonly showing disk util issues was asked to be suspended. there was some additional data showing that two other regions saw issues (at lower values), but still show a form of flit increase due to disk and cpu, which they were FOM'ed. waiting on value confirmation. 

11/14 - Blocker - None
Occurrences seem minimal. Data for client IPs that observed this also points to the same behavior, lower bitrate, but varying in cause (disk slow and ICP fwd slow, player issue, increase tat). 
Confirmed suggestions on how to proceed. Since there was some data showing that there was some instantaneous flit spikes on disk which correlated to some lower bit rates while the top bitrate was served off disk, suggested to mr suspend the HDD regions showing these issues in BT to see if we see major improvements along with setting expectations if occurrence is minimal. 

11/7 - Blocker - None 
Data in ticket suggests that hardware has played a role in reducing efficiency of these BT regions. However this hardware type is somewhat common across the network.  Also there was data showing during disk util BBC also had larger objects that would be served from disk (>2.1Mb chunks)
ERE-2422.root_cause: BBC noticed periods of degraded performance on their top bit rates within the BT ASN.  Analysis performed by akatec showed that there was a series of decays associated to serving from disk while disk UTIL / Flit usage during these times, all stemming from hardware using HDDs causing slow response and subsequently bitrate drops. This seemed to be isolated to these few HDD regions (which are due for an upgrade). FOMing was setup on these regions and has since put performance back within expected values.

ERE-1391.summary: REGION r21645 TAKES OVER THE TRAFFIC FROM r42816 {F-CS-7031313}
ERE-1391.mitigation_plan: NOCC ticket B-W-JD4R6R opened to follow up with ISP for any potential congestion/rexmit during peak hrs.
ERE-1391.executive_summary:
NOCC Ticket: https://issues.deploy.akamai.com/cgi-bin/view.cgi?issue_id=%23B-W-JD4R6R
------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
May 31st: Jubil (NIE) is following up again with the ISP to help them understand the RTT problem. Region r21645 is no longer mr-suspended, awaiting Akatec's response on the current perf analytics for the customer.

May 26th: Had a call with Jubil Padayambeth about the increased RTT issue towards PP from r42816. He is working with ISP to get an answer from their side.

May 25th: ISP wasn't able to reproduce the issue for higher RTT, Updated NOCC ticket with the latest evidence. Had a brief chat with PSG about the current status. Still awaiting his response.

May 18th: NOCC sent an update today that they along with NetServices still working with ISP to fix the latency issue from ISP(AS5713) side.

May 11th: NOCC Ext Request is raised to look into intermittent latency issues from TELKSA-JNB5 (r21645)

May 4th: Due to the suboptimal routing(high latency) within ISP's network, the score ramps up for r42816 intermittently. This behavior is evident for all considered PPs. As this is a problem with ISP's internal routing, the Off-Net region's reach to this PP is fine and their scores are stable. 
Advised to consult with NOCC so they can further check with ISP in order to fix this.
ERE-1391.root_cause: Due to incremental RTT with PP, r42816 is exhibiting higher scores intermittently. When that happens, r21645 was chosen, which was showing degraded perf.

ERE-1166.summary: { F-CS-6418962}  TLS handshake performance issue reported by BBC
ERE-1166.mitigation_plan: Fixing the Sky config should address the spiky f2b on the region.
ERE-1166.longterm_mitigation: Review the f2b for spiky traffic causing region overloads.
ERE-1166.executive_summary:
25/May/2023

-> Log analysis from multiple time-windows across regions pointed out to the Sky hostname "atlantis.epgsky.com" (e;e2) as the possible cause. Increase in hits 5x in a 10-second interval and with a very heavy flit demand leading to stability issues in the region.  Similar behavior was seen with another Sky hostname "entity.search.sky.com" (e;s114) on MR4265. 

-> Looking into the s114 MR traffic pattern from "entity.search.sky.com", we see a lot of origin failures and retries from midgress . 

-> Asked Akatec to reach out to Sky IAT with the above details to review/clarify what type of traffic does this hostname serve. The traffic on this hostname (CP508072) is spiky and has a high f2b causing region instability(UK regions). 


18/May/2023
Looked at the region health & logs for few of the regions which were repeatedly captured by the test-agent.
2 of the Maprules seem to have a very spiky flit demand and seen across multiple instances as reported by the agent. While we identify the spiky cpcode, lowered FOM on one of the regions r22076.

- Even with a lowered FOM of 50%, the region seems to have the same pattern from past 2 days (16th, 17th May).
- Reviewing the data, following MRs showed spiky traffic.
MR9441 - e;bg2.akamaiedge.net
MR 4265 - e;s114.akamaiedge.net

From log analysis, it seems to be one of the Sky Cpcodes.

11/May/2023
Issue: Client side timeouts/failures due to slow response
Analysis so far points to server running high on flit causing issues. However, it's not clear what's causing these flit spikes. As the issue is spread across multiple regions, we need to look at individual instances to identify which CPcode/traffic is leading to the flit spikes.

Awaiting logs from Akatec based on the recent test instances shared by customer's test agent.

Client Side PCAP shows delay between Client Hello and Server Hello.
ERE-1166.resolution_description:
Log analysis from multiple time-windows across regions pointed out two sky hostnames, as the possible cause for region overload. "atlantis.epgsky.com" (e;e2)  &  "entity.search.sky.com" (e;s114) 

Akatec is working with Sky IAT to fix the issue caused by origin failures which has resulted in this spiky traffic.

ERE-2902.summary: {F-CS-10234060} client source port modified to port 443 over QUIC protocol as seen from AK_CLIENT_PORT
ERE-2902.longterm_mitigation: Ghost release 22.2.4.1 with the proposed fix.
ERE-2902.executive_summary:
The issue is client source port is logged as 443 instead of one from an ephemeral port range between 1024 and 65535. hence the 3rd party WAF is denying the traffic on port 443, 
The issue is only reproducible on QUIC,

There were some updates to the QUIC, we are analyzing the issue, we may have to wait for a release to get this fixed hence we suggested to check with customer to disable the QUIC and they agreed to disable the Http3 and cname to akamai. We will share the ETA as soon as possible. 

Ghost release has been world deployed now.
ERE-2902.root_cause: The issue was client source port is logged as 443 instead of one from an ephemeral port range between 1024 and 65535. Client source port being forwarded to origin server as 443 which is being extracted from the AK_CLIENT_PORT built-in Variable at the client request stage and passed over the request header as Gclientport to origin server. There were some updates to the QUIC, which caused this issue,

ERE-2509.summary: 100x origin spike caused by an object becoming uncacheable intermittently
ERE-2509.executive_summary: The customer reported that from Dec 9th, they see a 100x increase in requests to the origin. There's a specific object that is being requested several times within a second as it becomes uncacheable intermittently. We initially thought it was the Age header, but the origin is not sending Age header and it's something that edge is adding in the response headers. We tried removing the Age header which hasn't resolved the issue. Since the origin is getting overloaded intermittently, we're looking for an option to mitigate the issue, and find the root cause for this behavior that started recently.

ERE-1376.root_cause: Some of the KDC machines were upgraded from OpenSSL 1.1 to OpenSSL 3.0 as part of a Ness s/w release. The keys generated on these new version machines were incompatible with the software Apple was using to generate the tokenids (while it works fine with OpenSSL). This resulted in clients outright rejecting the new tokenids, resulting in failure to authenticate with ProxyB.

ERE-1793.summary: {F-CS-7818861} Spotify:  US West Cloud Interconnect West Region latency.
ERE-1793.mitigation_plan:
Made changes to Delivery config to continue download the full object even when original client request got aborted.
Maprule suspended the sub-optimal regions.
ERE-1793.longterm_mitigation:
Pre-warming would be a better solution for this kind of content where client requesting for a segment of audio file while it takes few seconds to download entire file from origin.
Customer need to optimize the setup to support this kind of use-case.
ERE-1793.executive_summary: - US West Cloud Interconnect West Region latency
ERE-1793.resolution_description:
1) Disabled POC in CW and CI layers to store the full object in CW which can issue POC fragments to downstream. Added metadata tags to continue download the entire object even when the client aborts as that would result in cache-hit for subsequent requests for the same object.
2) Maprule suspended the problematic CI regions and reached out to AppNetRE team for res-structing CI map allowlist.
ERE-1793.root_cause:
Couple of issues
1) High origin load - Since origin doesn't support range requests, it is required to do a full object GET from the origin even when client does the range request. This increases the load towards origin compared to Edge load.
2) Spike in Timeouts - One of the CI regions have performance problems due to old HW and less number of machines causing slow response resulting in timeouts.

ERE-2093.summary: Purge seqnum behind because purge skipped due to over limit
ERE-2093.executive_summary:
This behavior can be mitigated by setting OPTIONS requests explicitly No-Store as the requests for OPTIONS methods are not cached, they are not explicitly considered no-store by the Edge server.
Long term mitigation will be time consuming due to the involvement of Engineering followed by testing, 
Following up on https://track.akamai.com/jira/browse/GHOST-4617
ERE-2093.root_cause:
This error can be seen for cacheable content when a parent server returns an out-of-date purge sequence ID for a request from a child server. However, the requests for OPTIONS methods are not cached, they are not explicitly considered no-store by the Edge server, and can trigger this error. 
Since the requests are not retried, the client receives the error.
For detailed analysis and mitigation, need to followup on a ghost ticket https://track.akamai.com/jira/browse/GHOST-4617

ERE-2504.summary: Intermittent handshake fail in C2G since 2024-12-10 06:51
ERE-2504.mitigation_plan: Akatec/PSG has confirmed that they can no longer reproduce the issue after upgrading the 59 machines in Japan. One machine still runs the old software and may remain suspended.
ERE-2504.longterm_mitigation: Akatec/PSG has confirmed that they can no longer reproduce the issue after upgrading the 59 machines in Japan. One machine still runs the old software and may remain suspended.
ERE-2504.executive_summary:
Context
The first attempt to enable the 0-RTT with TCP behavior (GHOST-4365) resulted in SI-22038. At this time, the version match on the rollout snippet was 11.6.4. The issue seen was caused by incorrect handling of unexpected error codes which was fixed in GREL-3306 (11.7.0.1). As part of 11.7.0.1 we also fixed telemetry around handshake failures where genuine failure cases where not being accounted for. During the re-rollout of GHOST-4365 we observed the error codes being handled correctly but we discovered more handshake failures. We reverted the rollout. The issue was tracked as GREL-3390 and the fix was submitted to 11.7.2.1.
 
GREL-3390 - There are certain (expected and normal) scenarios where the server write in the handshake may fail, such as due to congestion, network issues, record framing issues, etc. This is typically handled via a re-write and Ghost was erroneously trying to execute its early data flow.
 
The issue seen in the logs is not mTLS related but it was triggered when write fails in Ghost and Ghost needs to retry. The fix for this is also GREL-3390 (11.7.2.1).
 
RCA – Issue was seen on strangler machines during the rollout because the version on these machines were 11.7.2 > so the feature got enabled without the fix. The rollout snippet had a version match pointed to 11.6.4, while the correct match is 11.7.2.1/11.7.2.
 
Corrective Measures
Akatec/PSG has confirmed that they can no longer reproduce the issue after upgrading the 59 machines in Japan. One machine still runs the old software and may remain suspended.
 
Learnings
Scope in stranglers during rollouts. The version match should be reflective of the most stable/feature-target version.
ERE-2504.resolution_description:
Context
The first attempt to enable the 0-RTT with TCP behavior (GHOST-4365) resulted in SI-22038. At this time, the version match on the rollout snippet was 11.6.4. The issue seen was caused by incorrect handling of unexpected error codes which was fixed in GREL-3306 (11.7.0.1). As part of 11.7.0.1 we also fixed telemetry around handshake failures where genuine failure cases where not being accounted for. During the re-rollout of GHOST-4365 we observed the error codes being handled correctly but we discovered more handshake failures. We reverted the rollout. The issue was tracked as GREL-3390 and the fix was submitted to 11.7.2.1.
 
GREL-3390 - There are certain (expected and normal) scenarios where the server write in the handshake may fail, such as due to congestion, network issues, record framing issues, etc. This is typically handled via a re-write and Ghost was erroneously trying to execute its early data flow.
 
The issue seen in the logs is not mTLS related but it was triggered when write fails in Ghost and Ghost needs to retry. The fix for this is also GREL-3390 (11.7.2.1).
ERE-2504.root_cause: Issue was seen on strangler machines during the rollout because the version on these machines were 11.7.2 > so the feature got enabled without the fix. The rollout snippet had a version match pointed to 11.6.4, while the correct match is 11.7.2.1/11.7.2.

ERE-1310.summary: Read and connection timeouts corelating to high CPU load and memory Read errors.
ERE-1310.mitigation_plan:
As CPU is seen the bottle neck resource most of the times, FOM reduction would help addressing the issue.
As most of the mapping happen based on Fallback for Limelight ASN, added PPs to the NServers referred in Topology.xml
ERE-1310.longterm_mitigation:
Here, With 2-leg config setup, one edge map is forwarding the traffic to another Edge map hostname considered as Origin for Leg1 which is unusual. Suggestion is better to follow the best practices and get in touch with Product Management if needed.
Given the traffic is mostly concentrated with one Edge region at a time, serial pre-spread on both Leg1 and Leg2 hostnames will help in spreading the traffic within region.
ERE-1310.resolution_description:
Given the traffic is mostly concentrated with one Edge region at a time, serial pre-spread on both Leg1 and Leg2 hostnames will help in spreading the traffic within region.
As CPU is seen the bottle neck resource most of the times, FOM reduction would help addressing the issue.
As most of the mapping happen based on Fallback for Limelight ASN, added PPs to the NServers referred in Topology.xml
ERE-1310.root_cause:
Here its is the 2-Leg Config Setup used and the issue is seen with Leg1 response time.
Ghosts are getting flit full during peak hours with CPU 100% utilized, likely seen to be the cause for Read timeouts.
Due to the TRANSIT links becoming full on peak hours, RM triggers the link safety and reduces the capacity of the region to control the load on the link. This makes the traffic to suddenly shift from one region to another region and hence forward regions, causing the ghosts to get overloaded.

ERE-2979.summary: Intermittent ERR_READ_ERROR|fwdStateFree possibly due to ERR_CONNECT_TIMEOUT
ERE-2979.mitigation_plan: Provided the mitigation tag for fallback using HTTP status codes for OIPACL tier.
ERE-2979.executive_summary: Customer reported intermittent 503s due to a read error. These read errors with a "forward state free" message seem to be related to GHOST-4878/ ERE-2837. Since the bug fix is expected to take a month, hence provided the short term mitigation tags and the tag configuration needs approval from the concerned team,
ERE-2979.root_cause: Customer reported intermittent 503s due to a read error. These read errors with a "forward state free" message seem to be related to GHOST-4878, SIACL primary map would fail with just a HTTP 5xx status code, and this would result in no fallback request as the acceptance criteria for SIACL-ALT does not contain HTTP status codes. This would then result in Edge/TD0 request failing with the error code ERR_READ_ERROR|fwd_state_free.

ERE-1586.summary: Performance degradation(VPF, VSF) on Peacock and OneApp {F-CS-7422652}
ERE-1586.mitigation_plan: Release target for GREL-1872 is GHOST-11.2.5. Which fixed the issue.
ERE-1586.longterm_mitigation: Release target for GREL-1872 is GHOST-11.2.5. Which fixed the issue.
ERE-1586.executive_summary:
We did the query and coredump analysis for the time period between 8/1 18:26 and 8/2 2:00 UTC time, details are saved in the attached file queries.txt and coredump.txt. Here is the summary result:

1. Query shows we had major 256 roll spikes during this period. Almost all the 256 rolls had the same backtrace, containing "resources::FlitEngine::statCountHit"
2. We sampled 10 random machines  with valid core from top 3 rolling regions. All coredumps pointed to the same customer "business-api.tiktok.com" with cpcode 1140569
3. From Luna, we found cpcode 1140569 had rather choppy traffic during last 7 days. It also experienced 4xx spike on 08/01

Our conclusion is: tiktok traffic caused massive 256 rolls, which reduced the FLIT capacities of affected regions. The reduced capacities further impacted the NBCUniversal traffic
ERE-1586.root_cause: Tiktok traffic caused massive 256 rolls, which reduced the FLIT capacities of affected regions. The reduced capacities further impacted the NBCUniversal traffic

ERE-2433.summary: Ghost did not start using newly added AWS IP addresses
ERE-2433.executive_summary:
On Oct 16th and Oct 24th, Expedia experienced an incident where Akamai (Parent Servers)connected to 2 origin IPs, of a total of 6 they return in their AWS CNAME, causing overloads in the 2 AWS IPs.

ERE-1137.summary: AMD: Corrupted gzipped cache generated by ghost
ERE-1137.executive_summary:
Currently we have no quick way to avoid this rare occurrence.
crc and length error happens on 2353 object which suggests some faulty hardware behavior or disk/router on top tier Akamai gHost which does compression for first time when we receive full size object.
As delivery and origin fetch is HTTPS, MITM proxies should not be able to interfere. HexDump diff is not suggestive of bit-flip either.
if their AWS origin want to send compressed object that would be good to save bandwidth as well as we don't need to compress. Akamai can advertise gzip support header via (<edgeservices:lma.origin-edge>) to Origin.
https://ac-aloha.akamai.com/home/ccdp-ps-body-of-knowledge. XABI is not needed but we can use DBI (disk-block-integrity) for manifest CP (We don't know if this can fix rare occurrence completely but its good to have seeing problem faced here)
ERE-1137.resolution_description:
Auto recovery as it a rare event.
Disk-block-integrity can be used but we don't know if this happens again.
ERE-1137.root_cause:
Rare occasion of corruption while doing gzip in Akamai. 
Not able to trace root cause but we have identified it happens inside Akamai.

ERE-910.summary: Newly added regions caused high rebuffering to end users
ERE-910.mitigation_plan: Short-term mitigation was to have the MR suspensions in-place until the fix for ghost-roll was available.
ERE-910.longterm_mitigation: ghost release "10.10.0" - including the fix for tcmalloc(GHOST-2340)
ERE-910.executive_summary:
3/Nov/2022
- Next phase of testing scheduled on Nov3rd. Awaiting customer confirmation.

27/Oct/2022
r45521 remained stable with the SOIP traffic. No buffering reported.
Next Action: Testing non-SOIP traffic in the region in 2 phases.

20/Oct/2022
No tcmalloc rolls since the ghost release "10.10.0" ( World Deploy was complete on 17th Oct)
r45521 was unsuspended for testing on 19th/Oct, serving traffic ~17GMT.
No issues reported by Sky so far.

13/Oct/2022
10.10.0 - Phase III is complete.  World Deploy on 17th Oct
https://release.akamai.com/r4/#/networks/FF/releases/15662
- Still awaiting test window from Customer.

29/Sep/2022
- Test window is not finalized yet.
- Awaiting Customer.

22/Sep/2022
- IAT is awaiting response from Sky on the test window. 
- The tcmalloc(GHOST-2340) related fixes are now going into as part of 10.9.5 (Previously was targeted into 10.10.0 - Mid of oct) which is tentatively going to start WD on Sep 28th.
https://track.akamai.com/jira/browse/ERE-906

Blocker: test window from customer

15/Sep/2022
- There is an emergency change freeze due to the death of HM The Queen, until 19th Sept(tentative date and subject to change).
Next Action: Awaiting test window from Sky.

8/Sep/2022
- There is no concrete data which shows the rebuffering was caused due to server issues/ghost rolls. 
- The session data from Conviva doesn't correlate with any slow requests from Akamai Edge. The "gaps" in subsequent client requests, could the possible reason for rebuffering. As there are no server logs for the rebuffering session, it could be that the connection was never established.

Action-item:
un-suspend region 45521 and maprule suspend all non-SOIP traffic for the region, letting the traffic from SOIP be served without any caps. If there are any issues noticed, Akatec to collect following information for futher analysis:
During planned time of testing
- Un-suspend 45521 without any caps on that region
- Monitor re-buffering metric in Conviva
- If re-buffering is seen, engage NOCC to suspend the region again.
- Review if there is any ghost rolls in the region, and request NOCC to rename core dump for the machines that had ghost rolls.
Perform usual troubleshooting and identify the machine (within r45521) likely causing it and confirm it co-relates with ghost roll.
Collect ddc, cache and ghostmon logs around the time of re-buffering.

Next Action: Awaiting test window from Sky.

1/Sep/2022
- There were 3 different issues that contributed to the rebuffering spike and ghost roll was one of them. However we wouldn't know what was the impact caused by these ghost rolls alone, as these regions were suspended before the config was fixed.
- Few ghost rolls seen in r45520, r45521, r45533 are due to "tcmalloc: allocation failures", which seems be related to GHOST-2340.
- These regions are now back online with a bitcap limited to 10G since Aug 25th. No issues reported since. 
- As a best practice, suggested to bypass icp for FailAction requests.
- The  roll rate doesn't seem to be significant, looks the same.
ERE-910.resolution_description: No tcmalloc rolls since the ghost release "10.10.0" ( World Deploy was completed on 17th Oct). 2 regions were unsuspended for testing and no issues were reported by customer, post this fix.
ERE-910.root_cause:
Ghost rolls on the 3 newly added regions.
Ghost rolls due to "tcmalloc" (which is one of the reasons seen in this case) is common across the platform however concentrated on X7P & BISON hardware, which the ghost team is aware of.

ERE-3082.mitigation_plan: Enabled RA fallback instead of SR for Origin IPACL as a short term mitigation
ERE-3082.longterm_mitigation: The permanent fix for the SR2 map will be a fix needed in ghost, https://track.akamai.com/jira/browse/GHOST-7069 has been raised for the same.
ERE-3082.executive_summary:
IFOOD.COM faced 503  "ERR_READ_ERROR fwdStateFree" during Black Friday
As per the provided logs, the issue looks similar to the SI-00023285. It does not make any forward to the parent, it detected the bad parent/region, 
We can use the provided workaround the ERE-3057.
ERE-3082.root_cause: The Region health detects mid-tier regions as bad and origin IPACL has no fallback mechanism even with Acceptance criteria enabled. Actually, the Edge normally uses two tier to go forward: SIACL (primary) and SIACL-ALT (backup). SIACL tries to provide two forward paths, but both get removed because the ghost marks those regions as bad while SIACL-ALT provides one forward path, but it has strict acceptance criteria, so Ghost is not allowed to use it. With no valid forward paths left, Ghost does not trigger any forward request at all, which leads to the observed errors.

ERE-2274.summary: NBC Peacock observed spikes in high latency between Media Tailor and ORD regions during Olympics
ERE-2274.executive_summary:
8/2 - 
Blocker: None
Incident is in phase2 -
Overloading regions which caused slowness and subsequently MT to clientabort after 3s is attributed to RA underestimating demand for these MT mpgs on w16 because it is flit heavy load. Currently they are looking into how we can work around this / prevent this for future events. (there was also seen small surges in traffic that lined up with one of the client abort cases, which seems to be some bad retry logic MT has, NBC is currently looking into this)


The regions that MT most commonly goes to have been capped and we have seen no issues since. 

We are also pursuing further MPG splitting to be more accurate, was uncovered that the topology file contained  their MT client IPs to split MPG, but these IPs are not the NS thus was not effective.  Seeing now if EDNS0 can be used to do clientIP mapping from the route52 dns ips these MT clients are using.
ERE-2274.resolution_description:
FOM top X regions being hit by a limited amount of ec2 instances.

currently investigating other avenues for longer term, but most point towrds putting this workflow on a separate host and map
ERE-2274.root_cause: Over load on regions caused requests to slowdown for MT. Caused by oscillating load at the RA level where demand estimates did not match up with load on regions. Some of these cases caused overload and issues with MT delivery (ad insertion workflow which uses the manifest and inserts custom ads) when requests slowed down.

ERE-2974.summary: Same ghost server mapped for fail-action and quick-retry
ERE-2974.longterm_mitigation: Products to review fallback mapping strategy. Options could include using second hop SRO for FA instead of SCM.
ERE-2974.resolution_description: None available in the short term.
ERE-2974.root_cause: That's a known caveat. MCH and FA mapping using two different mapping strategies. The former relies on SRO, while the latter uses fallback mapping akin to RA mapping. There's no shared state of information between the two. The forward serial hashing for MCH and FA mapping use different serial ranges, but given a single machine serves multiple serials, this could still lead to corner cases where not just the parent region, but the same machine could be handed out for a small subset of requests leading to this behavior. FA/QR isn't expected to be 100% efficient with the current design. There are product level discussions on how to address these, but I don't expect this to change anytime soon.

ERE-2412.executive_summary:
Nov: 14:
Migration plan is being discussed (over mail)between SNI-Devs and IAT

Nov 7:
This issue was escalated to SNID SREs and Devs due to the suboptimal behaviour (it was unable to spread the load well enough within the region compared to earlier presperead + trip-rr). It was identified that Coupang's hostnames were not in the hostname+serial map before the switch, so SNId had no control over in-region load-bal and it was just based upon classic mapping (but without prespread+rr).

Awaiting response from devs that what should be the standard process before such migrations in future.

Meanwhile, SNI slot 252191 partial prespread (minGhost=16) is completed under MAPPINGENG-6442
ERE-2412.root_cause: Coupang's high traffic slots were prespread and TRIP RRed to handle the high number of requests. After migration from 2 VIP to single SNI slot, the in-region loadbal couldn’t keep up (and Trip-RR and PS were also not there).

ERE-1604.longterm_mitigation: Network team is working on contingency plans (based on conversation with the transit provider for capacity upgrade) to add additional capacity in 3 weeks (From webex chat)
ERE-1604.executive_summary:
Aug 24:
Summary: Due to congestion on upstream international links of ISP:Workonline - 2 public ecors TERACO-CPT2, JINX-JNB had to be capped. This reduced the public capacity in the South Africa causing OOC edge mapping. NOCC and Networks team are working with the ISP to monitor the links and gradually increasing the caps as and when the ISP's issues are getting fixed. Upgrades are planned for the ISP/ecors in about 3-4 weeks.
Blocker: None, saw some high penalties on some ZA regions, checking with map-ops if anything can be done from that side to reduce the ooc mapping.
ERE-1604.resolution_description: NOCC is working with the ISP to get the issue addressed with the ISP ( B-V-4PKZS74 )
ERE-1604.root_cause: OOC mapping seen at South Africa due to 2 suspensions/capping at regions in 2 public ecors TERACO-CPT2, JINX-JNB . The reason for suspension/capping is the transit ISP for the ecor, WORKONLINE_TRANSIT have faced an undersea cable cut in their international links leading to congestion of traffic out of South Africa. To address the RHM and forward error alerts, the region had to be suspended or capped by NOCC. This reduced the available edge capacity for many end user ISPs that relied on the public ecor and hence OOC mapping was seen.

We have multiple occurrences of “ERR_CONNECT_TIMEOUT|commConnectTimeout” between Region 46557:SDN-SIN to Customer Origin 

NOCC confirmed that region is overloaded and checking ghost logs when flit usage was high , there are high number of hits for Appsflyer hostname.

There's an ongoing ERE ticket addressing region overload caused due to this customer :  it looks like appsflyer* hostname is generating lot of traffic (around 2 million hps) and that has resulted in overloading on many FF regions.

ERE-1037.summary: {F-CS-6313362 } Zero size cache js objects were returned with 200 status to clients in 20 minutes
ERE-1037.mitigation_plan:
Use Systems properly.
TTL should be adjusted for non changing files in days.
ECT can be removed from object like CSS/JS
ERE-1037.executive_summary:
Issue should not be persistent as it requires content-tag purge delay + origin ERR_CHUNK_INCOMPLETE
JS objects should have higher TTLs
content tag can be avoided for common popular objects too.
Aggressive purging is by customer is miss use, they should adopt TTL based eviction in general 

Docs: 
https://techdocs.akamai.com/purge-cache/reference/rate-limiting
https://techdocs.akamai.com/purge-cache/docs/cache-strategies
ERE-1037.root_cause: Aggressive purging caused one machine to lag which prompted refresh via peer and got ERR_CHUNK from origin so for few second object was not delivered till it was refreshed properly

ERE-1575.summary: DSS traffic spikes causing VST/bitrate change because of heavy load on single region in France
ERE-1575.mitigation_plan:
When the region was FOM-Capped, higher RTTs vanished. The region was uncapped for a few days to trace the fwd route/throughput from different regions but no anomaly was found. 

It seems the issue was intermittent, the region is capped at 0.7 FOM.
ERE-1575.executive_summary:
Aug 17:
Uncapped the FOM for 4 days to check any congestion/routing issues on the forward (link) side, there were none. Asked if there are any other occurrences from the customer. The region is capped back at 0.7 FOM.

Aug 10:
Region r41892 served an abundance of CLIENT_ABORTs at two reported instances in July. Upon checking there was a sudden surge in the region's ingress traffic, which was generated by Quick-Retry(Low throughput/ICP requests failed due to suspended machines in the region and went to the Parent tier).
As a mitigation, the region is FOM Capped to 70%.

There is no evidence on the link side showing congestion/routing issue ended in throughput drop and excessive QRs, however, after the FOM Cap, High RTT values disappeared.

Uncapping the FOM for a day to check the Link's state for any congestion/suboptimal routing.
ERE-1575.root_cause:
In both occurrences, the sudden inbound traffic was caused due to excessive QRs due to the drop in the forward throughput. At the edge(r41892), most of the requests were failing over ICP (due to overloaded machines) and went forward to TD0. However, from the link's dashboards, there was not any visible capacity issue, which further indicated some fwd-side intermittent slowness (probably congestion or routing change).
Traces were set up to the region r41892 from Paris and other EU regions but no anomaly was noticed during the test.

ERE-2568.summary: Timeouts from FABRIC-BOM regions during Ind-Eng T20 - 31 Jan 2025
ERE-2568.executive_summary: On January 31st, 2025, during the England Tour of India T20I 04 of 05, MCO observed a spike in HTTP 5xx errors affecting Hindi stream delivery around 13:25 UTC. Investigation revealed that HTTP 504 errors were caused by timeouts from specific CW regions (FABRIC-BOM1t and FABRIC-BOM1s) towards SSAI Origin servers. A sudden spike in CPU utilization in these regions was noted, but no corresponding flit spikes were observed. Additionally, multiple suspend flags were identified during the issue timeframe. NOCC T3 confirmed that the suspend flags resulted from GHosts being unable to contact cryptoservers, rather than CPU or network load, and suggested to further check with ERE on this.
ERE-2568.root_cause: Due to the ECOR FABRIC -BOM connectivity /increased retransmissions failures/timeouts were seen.

ERE-1833.summary: CBS Preparation for the Super Bowl'2024
ERE-1833.longterm_mitigation:
Review AT&T mapping & perf within US on event-map. 
Competitive analysis indicates ttlb on Akamai is not on par with Fastly. Action item: Review CBS config/map perf for both live/vod map.
ERE-1833.executive_summary:
1st Feb
----------------
Due to capacity constraints within US, streaming the event on a higher priority was an option. IAT seems to have worked this out with mgmt and the latest event from Jan-28th was streamed on w42 with P5. 
----------------

25th Jan
---------------
Poor perf during the 14th-Jan event. Lost traffic share. Findings indicate higher OOC mapping for AT&T.
IAT to work with Network planning on AT&T capacity in US.
---------------

18/Jan
---------------
Latest from the call - 17th-Jan
- Akamai perf was bad compared with Fastly. Avg ttfb was ~250ms to 350ms whereas Fastly was around 200ms consitently.
- Traffic on the Event Map on 14th was significant, resulting in OOC mapping.
- Log analysis indicates ooc mapping with high transfer times. Server tat looks good.
- No baseline data to indicate perf-degradation. Customer expectation is to have ttlb on par with Fastly. 

11/Jan
--------------
Latest from yesterday's call:
- Akamai performance looks good for Live Test-Object
- Event review and map discussions - complete. 
- Discussion on whether to use CW - based on Fastly's MediaShield cap/limits - in progress

4/Jan
--------------
- Reviewed the stats from 31st-Dec. Doppler results indicate 'Test-Object for Live' performs better than Fastly. 

28/Dec
--------------
Next Action:  To review the performance with dscw16 map for the event on 24th-Dec. Will be discussed over the call today.

20/Dec
--------------
Before the actual event review, CBS IAT is working with the customer on finalizing traffic share for Akamai. 
Akamai perf is not on par with Fastly/Paramount as per cedixis/doppler test results. 
So far, poor TAT is linked to higher flit overload. Investigation in Progress
Next Step: IAT to work with customer on config update to have the doppler test on "dscw16" event map rather than "z".
--------------
ERE-1833.resolution_description:
Standard Event Recommendations. Event was delivered on a p5 map due to capacity constraint in the US.
Event peaked at ~28Tbps. No major issues reported.

ERE-2110.summary: Rebuffering issue from a JP region 45865 which is for ch646 parent map
ERE-2110.longterm_mitigation: With the addition of addition of 2 new JP regions, the allowlist issue should be mitigated.
ERE-2110.executive_summary:
9th May
- No active action items.
- check with map-ops on allowlist update procedure in region decon scenario, to avoid similar issues.
ERE-2110.resolution_description:
FOM was lowered as short-term mitigation.
This seems to be a corner case where 2 JP regions got decon'd at the same time. Even though it's was a large mpg, a single JP region 45865 was able to fit the entire demand. The issue here was due to the sudden spike in map-point demand, which caused flit overload and not entirely an allowlist issue.

Now with the addition of addition of 2 new JP regions, the allowlist issue should be mitigated. The KT regions are not allowed to serve the NS and hence JP is preferred by mapper. In addition, JP has better score compared to KR from the US.
ERE-2110.root_cause: The issue here was due to the sudden spike in map-point demand, which caused flit overload and not entirely an allowlist issue.

ERE-2746.summary: AMD | Multiple reports of 5xx from ZEE since march'25 due to RJIL connectivity issues.
ERE-2746.mitigation_plan: Move TD0 from "chapac/chapaf" to  "zch3/zch4" pair which doesn't have any RJIL-AANP regions in the allowlist.
ERE-2746.longterm_mitigation: Work with ISP & Networks on addressing the issues on the primary path within JIO.
ERE-2746.executive_summary:
-  22/May/25
RJIL connectivity issues towards public regions (TD - OS).
- Upcoming Event this weekend. IAT would be testing this on "zch3" and "chapacf" as TD0-pair. Idea here is to not use Jio-AANP regions on the forward tiers. 
- IAT to work with OSRE team to review the map.

Zee has been reporting multiple instance of 5xx spike in errors seen on their AMD setup with MSL4 as Origin. What has been observed from multiple round of analysis is that we see RJIL connectivity issues towards public regions. No common region pattern so far.
ERE-2746.resolution_description:
For the upcoming event, suggested moving TD0 from "chapac/chapaf" to  "zch3/zch4" pair which doesn't have any RJIL-AANP regions in the allowlist.
Recommend working with Networks & the ISP on addressing the issue on the primary path.
ERE-2746.root_cause: Multiple instances of 5xx observed b/w Edge-TD (live-linear stream/ no backup) due to connectivity issues in JIO backbone.

ERE-2088.summary: NBC Reported Video Start Failures in Conviva On Verizon ASN 701(Acorss multiple states in the East Coast)
ERE-2088.executive_summary:
NBC Reported Video Start Failures in Conviva Only On Verizon ASN 701(Acorss multiple states in the East Coast)
VSF reports from customer's monitoring tool(conviva) suggests VSF were caused by DNS failures.
This caused Devices on Verizon ISP unable to launch Peacock app

VSF lasted almost 4 days as shown in conviva screenhot before subsiding

Issue start time: Friday 04/12/2024 at 3:00 AM ET (7:00 AM GMT)

Issue end time: Tuesday 04/16/2024 at 2:37 AM ET( 6:37 AM GMT)
ERE-2088.root_cause:
AS701, VZ, set an incorrect MTU setting in a few locations, going from Jumbo to normal packet size, though went to 1514 instead of 1500.  
Change lined up and seen to have caused undesired effect and caused a series of sporadic NS failures as VZ end users hit issue links in VZ looking up Peacocktv hostname through an anycasted auth ns setup on akam.

ERE-3022.summary: High SSL errors in midgress causes 503 spikes with high flits
ERE-3022.mitigation_plan:
FOM - which can be reverted 
Change SS map to something close to their old setup which was not subject to these issues. 
Prespread for serial they are using.
ERE-3022.longterm_mitigation: requested customer fix retry logic to prevent congestive retrying.
ERE-3022.executive_summary:
11/6 - blocker - pending actions
OVerload at SS layer causing an increase in 5xx - FOM was setup and helped a bit but not fully.  Confirmed Tues that the 5xx actually lines up fully with SS map change. Shows issues with a smaller sized caribou machines.


	1.	map change 11/6 ~16utc - https://track.akamai.com/jira/browse/AKAMAPSPL-1840 
	2.	prespread  - 11/6 ~15:30 utc - https://track.akamai.com/jira/browse/MAPPINGENG-7893
3. FOMs are still in place for now. 


- Fom change helped but not as effective as we need (worst region still overloading at 50%
- testing prespreading at largest edge region
- adjusting SS map
ERE-3022.resolution_description: combo of removing caibou from SS map to mimic the older SS map which didnt have these issues, and the addition of pre-spreading.
ERE-3022.root_cause:
Observed was a mix of customers aggressive  retries 3x3 due to loopback /two tree logic, so within seconds a region could get 9 retries at SS layer per request that is failing.  Additionally the region causing the most slowness seemed to be a caribou which slowed down and eventually failed service checks even after FOM cap of 50%  - https://track.akamai.com/jira/browse/GHOST-6421 


for details analysis see 
https://track.akamai.com/jira/browse/ERE-3022?focusedId=11775207&page=com.atlassian.jira.plugin.system.issuetabpanels%3Acomment-tabpanel#comment-11775207

ERE-2169.summary: Sequence Id on Edge Greater than Max Sequence
ERE-2169.resolution_description: Follow up in SYSPURGSUP-269 and review high frequency purging behaviour and caching strategy.
ERE-2169.root_cause: Jetsagent updates on parent was split second slower than child and requests occurred between both timings.

ERE-1052.summary: {F-CS-6412649} PCH region in US getting mapped to SS parent in EU despite SS map contains regions in USA
ERE-1052.mitigation_plan: Asked the PSG team to engage Site Shield map-ops to add appropriate US regions in the MR - 10831's allowlist that have sufficient link cap. to accommodate the demand so the map-point assignment stays in country.
ERE-1052.executive_summary:
Nov 10 - 

SS map-ops asked to add more regions for MR - 10831.


Nov 3 -

The issue is due to insufficient link cap to handle the Big Map-point demand, as traffic to both the local regions is over the same link. PSG to engage SS map-ops to add more regions with sufficient link cap for the SS maprule 10831.


PSG - 
US located SS regions not selected by PCH region in US despite better score and load seems correct
ERE-1052.root_cause: The cause for the OOCM was the insufficient link capacity for the ECOR (as validated from MLE dashboard) to handle the high MPG demand for the MR - 10831. The traffic during US peak demand hours was hence mapped via a different link to UK/EU SS regions.

ERE-1060.mitigation_plan: Advised reported to work with AMG team to resolve the issue.
ERE-1060.longterm_mitigation: Shared KB article regarding the same for future such use cases.

ERE-1193.summary: 504 Timeout issue caused by a tier 1 region
ERE-1193.mitigation_plan: Since the problem was evident on X7p HW, Both X7p regions were swapped with X8s with the help of MapOps(GSPMAPSTRA-1936). Requested MapOps to make a comment in BLC for similar future replacements such as during decon of those X8 regions.
ERE-1193.longterm_mitigation:
1:) TikTok's traffic compatibility with X7Ps (for those specific rolls) is being looked at under GHOST-1734 and GHOST-2477 but the problem is not widespread so a fix-release can be delayed.

2:) Since PCH is not a viable solution, as per the CW product team, that is not a product fit due to the nature of traffic (TikTok images are tiny in size but very large in numbers). This specific use case for CW can be reviewed.

3:) The config should be armed with resiliency features to deal with these TD failures
ERE-1193.executive_summary:
Jan 31:
As per MapOps for the request to swap X7p regions with X8,  "Hi Ankit, I will add this topic to our new sprint (this and next week). Unless there are any blockers changes should be in ~Wednesday next week (Feb 8th)."

Jan 25:
MapOps ticket GSPMAPTIX-1600 is opened to swap X7p regions with X8 for PCH Maprule MR4595-MR4599. Currently, there are two X7p regions (r36138 and r41407) in primary tiers.
ERE-1193.root_cause:
TD1 in the config is a 5-way shared (MR4595-4599) SPCH maprule. Each shard has 6 regions and by the design of the maps, there is just one region in the primary, and the rest 5 others are in the overflow tier.

The primary tier regions for shards MR4596 and MR4597 were r36138 and r41407, built upon X7p HW.

Due to TikTok's traffic conflicting with an X7p HW (Ghost bug GHOST-1734, GHOST-2477), both regions were having severe stability issues and rolling frequently.

On the other hand, the config was lacking resiliency features e.g: FA to deal with G2G failures.

ERE-3057.mitigation_plan: Team has enabled RA fallback instead of SR for Origin IPACL as a short term mitigation
ERE-3057.longterm_mitigation: The permanent fix for the SR2 map will be a fix needed in ghost, https://track.akamai.com/jira/browse/GHOST-7069
ERE-3057.executive_summary:
We enabled debug logging CLIENT, FWD, FWD_SL, HTTP and analysed the logs. 
Based upon our analysis, we saw more than 3k hits/sec to a machine. Hence we suggested to enable the serial prespread if this can help us somehow https://track.akamai.com/jira/browse/MAPPINGENG-7943
Upon further  analysis with Engineering with more debug logging, we conclude that the Region health detects mid-tier regions as bad and origin IPACL has no fallback mechanism even with Acceptance criteria enabled.  
Team has enabled RA fallback instead of SR for Origin IPACL as a short term mitigation and we haven't seen error post that change. The permanent fix for the SR2 map will be a fix needed in ghost, https://track.akamai.com/jira/browse/GHOST-7069 has been raised for the same.
ERE-3057.root_cause: The Region health detects mid-tier regions as bad and origin IPACL has no fallback mechanism even with Acceptance criteria enabled. Actually, the Edge normally uses two tier to go forward: SIACL (primary) and SIACL-ALT (backup). SIACL tries to provide two forward paths, but both get removed because the ghost marks those regions as bad while SIACL-ALT provides one forward path, but it has strict acceptance criteria, so Ghost is not allowed to use it. With no valid forward paths left, Ghost does not trigger any forward request at all, which leads to the observed errors.

Property Config.

Caching
Force revalidation of stale objects: Serve stale if unable to validate -> Always revalidate with origin

Origin Failure Recovery Policy (both HLS and SMOOTH)
Enable Retry Before Recovery: Off -> On
 
Advanced Metadata
AKA_PM_BYPASS_TD = true -> false

ERE-2323.summary: FOSSL issue due to wrong forward origin hostname by Akamai
ERE-2323.mitigation_plan: Ask the customer not to send anything other than 200 OK for the URLs which trigger the RO
ERE-2323.longterm_mitigation: Ask the customer not to send anything other than 200 OK for the URLs which trigger the RO
ERE-2323.executive_summary:
FOSSL issue due to wrong origin host selected by RO supercache via MCH.
The origin is responding with 302 for the request from Akamai. RO does not support the 302 response by default, since the Origin is responding with a redirect, the Resource Optimizer Pearl is chasing the redirects, which is breaking customer configurations.

To fix this:
1: Ask the customer not to send anything other than 200 OK for the URLs which trigger the RO
OR
2: Enable Resource Optimizer Extended Compatibility in the Property Manager configuration instead of the regular RO behavior. RO Extended does not chase redirects, which fixes the FOSSL errors and other functions that may be broken while using the regular Resource Optimizer behavior.
ERE-2323.root_cause: The origin is responding with 302 for the request from Akamai. RO does not support the 302 response by default, since the Origin is responding with a redirect, the Resource Optimizer Pearl is chasing the redirects, which is breaking customer configurations.

ERE-2735.summary: Increase in 425 Too Early responses huge jump in errors on April 28, 2025 and ongoing (potentially started around April 22)
ERE-2735.mitigation_plan: Made PM change to add <security:ssl.tls-early-data.enabled-http-methods>GET</security:ssl.tls-early-data.enabled-http-methods> by default
ERE-2735.longterm_mitigation: Made PM change to add <security:ssl.tls-early-data.enabled-http-methods>GET</security:ssl.tls-early-data.enabled-http-methods> by default
ERE-2735.executive_summary:
Increase in 425 Too Early responses huge jump in errors on April 28, 2025 and ongoing (potentially started around April 22) 
The issue happened because of PM has not yet started sending the GET as a parameter for the tag (<security:ssl.tls-early-data.enabled-http-methods>) by default. They are planning to deploy the PM changes to send the GET request for that tag.
Customer confirmed that the issue has been mitigated after the rollout.
ERE-2735.root_cause:
PM did not start sending the GET as a parameter for the tag by default, 
Earlier 0-RTT's method was controlled by the ‘baseline’ tag: <network:tls-early-data.allowed-http-methods default=GET"> and used to be enabled by default but got deprecated in ghost 22.1.1 as per design. The new request tag: <security:ssl.tls-early-data.enabled-http-methods> does not have a default value in the code and can only be enabled via the updated property manager behavior (Early Data (0-RTT) Advanced).
