ERE-2707.summary: QR enabled for alt path on slow forwards but goes forward to MCH instead
ERE-2707.mitigation_plan: Requested to set this tag "AKA_PM_BYPASS_TD" to true,
ERE-2707.longterm_mitigation: Requested to set this tag "AKA_PM_BYPASS_TD" to true,
ERE-2707.executive_summary:
18th April 
Logs show a number of timeouts with ERR_READ_TIMEOUT and the requests failing are all going forward to peers with ICP requests. Requested team to disable the ICP 

29th April 
The ICP as a whole has been disabled in the configuration, the concerned team will be gathering the current stats from the customer.

1st May
Disabling ICP as a whole did not work as expected, after further analysis we found that the issue is because of custom TD metadata in advanced override without disabling the product TD metadata, hence to mitigate this issue, it is requested to set this tag "AKA_PM_BYPASS_TD" to true, 

7th May
After the metadata change, the QR is triggering as expected and the VPF is in within executable limit but the original forward and QR were timing out at the OS level, We are tracking the slowness issue at OS level under ANREACT-221
ERE-2707.root_cause: The issue was happened because of custom TD metadata in advanced override without disabling the product TD metadata, hence to mitigate this issue, it is requested to set this tag "AKA_PM_BYPASS_TD" to true,

ERE-1823.summary: Drop in TD0 offload for multiple customers
ERE-1823.mitigation_plan: A bunch of low storage capacity regions were swapped by LCS ones by MapOps
ERE-1823.longterm_mitigation: Hulu's TD0 (ch420-ch459) is being migrated to MCH.
ERE-1823.executive_summary:
Jan 18:
35% of migration will be completed by EOD (so far 27%). Over the last week, the TD0 hit rate has improved significantly.
Jan 11:
9% of Hulu's TD0 traffic has been moved to MCH till Jan 10. Another change from Mapops also pushed one change yesterday.
Jan 4:
Another change for regions swap is being pushed from MapOps today. Also, Hulu TD0 shards are being migrated to MCH from today.
https://collaborate.akamai.com/confluence/display/GPO/Change+rollout%3A+Migrate+OTT+Maps+to+MCH

Dec 28th:
No change was pushed last week, so followed up with mapops on GSPMAPSTRA-2839. Dilip is also not aware of mapops next push date.

Dec 21st:
Mapops is making gradual changes to have Hulu Shards bigger disk regions. The first change was pushed last week. Keeping this ticket on hold to track the offload status after a few iterations.

Dec 14th:
Microsoft migrated Xbox from Stackpath to Akamai on Nov 1. Microsoft's TD0 has overlapping regions from Hulu and Disney's TD0, which ended up eating cache space due to the large library size which is ~3x in terms of footprint compared to earlier, and left narrower room at TD0 for the rest large OTT customer's cache.
ERE-1823.root_cause:
A large shared TD0 space was taken by Microsoft after it migrated from Stackpath, as TD0 regions were shared across different customers' sharded TD0 maps.
Due to this, large OTT customers such as Hulu and Disney started seeing a drop in their offload.

ERE-1898.summary: Several customers complained about FWD_CERT errors after the installs
ERE-1898.mitigation_plan: Working as per the current design.
ERE-1898.longterm_mitigation: Working as per the current design. https://track.akamai.com/jira/browse/MAPPINGENG-4739 is filed to review the current design and based on the discussions on this ticket, we can take further actions.
ERE-1898.executive_summary: IBM, Apple and several other customers noticed large outages as we did not have origin certs available
ERE-1898.resolution_description: Working as per the current design.
ERE-1898.root_cause:
During the installation process, the machine is suspended, and approximately 30 minutes later, the first Ghost restart occurs. This is followed by a gap of about 30 minutes before commencing the new binary installation process.
During the new binary installation, it's evident from [3] that the certificate loading process requires additional time. From the moment "Ready to serve requests" is reached until the certificates are fully loaded, which takes couple of minutes (10+ minutes), clients are actively sending requests
Looking at load on all the Ghosts in the regions, all of them were decently loaded <30% flit. Yet, DNSP makes a decision to push traffic to the suspended machines

ERE-1881.summary: NBC Reported Repeated requests for the same segments to the origin within short period of time during NFL exclusive event
ERE-1881.mitigation_plan: The NBC PS team had put the config fix on staging on Jan 12 (v 189 on staging has the fix). However, this was not pushed to Prod at the time on Jan 12. Fix has still not been deployed to Production. PS team is working on this config fix.
ERE-1881.longterm_mitigation: The NBC PS team had put the config fix on staging on Jan 12 (v 189 on staging has the fix). However, this was not pushed to Prod at the time on Jan 12. Fix has still not been deployed to Production. PS team is working on this config fix.
ERE-1881.executive_summary:
NBC Reported Repeated requests for the same segments to the origin within short period of time during a live game on Dec 24th approx around 2023-12-24T02:17 to 2023-12-24T02:19 UTC. 
Customer temperature is elevated as they have a large event coming up this weekend (Jan 13th and 14th) and there is concern that this repeated requests for the same segments to the origin could impact their media origin.
Cloud-Wrapper did not offer offer expected offload for these segments as we see same segment go forward to origin multiple time from same CW machine.
The NBC PS team had put the config fix on staging on Jan 12 (v 189 on staging has the fix). However, this was not pushed to Prod at the time on Jan 12. Fix has still not been deployed to Production. PS team is working on this config fix.
ERE-1881.root_cause:
You can't combine the SureRoute or Tiered Distribution behaviors with Cloud Wrapper.
There were timeouts to TD0 parent, which triggered the normal fail-action. The QR implementation doesn't specify an alternate parent, but skips tier to directly go to CW layer again.
The the fail-action setup adds a header to force cache to be bypassed so that the error previously cached are not served.  However the bypass logic is being misapplied to the CW tiers too
This would explain to an extent the anomaly seen where same CW goes forward multiple times for same segment.

ERE-2599.summary: Cache hit ratio decreased due to pre-spread serial edge going to peer increased
ERE-2599.mitigation_plan: SNId config re-relese with the correct hostname serial association
ERE-2599.longterm_mitigation: Some checks should be in-place from the dev side to avoid such issues
ERE-2599.executive_summary: Roku observed a decrease in cache hit ratio around 16 GMT on Feb 11th across multiple cpcodes on their Grafana dashboard through Trafficpeak. It went from 70+% to ~50% and hasn't recovered since then. Reviewing logs, we see that the requests were logged with pre-spread serial and many edge servers served specific ARL where all of them went forward to peer causing increased requests forward, resulting in reduced cache hit ratio.
ERE-2599.root_cause: Serial misconfig under SNId hostname config file "control.snid.snitoserial.freeflow"

ERE-2018.summary: Disney+ | Charter ASN 20115 metrics degraded on Akamai across US regions
ERE-2018.mitigation_plan: Investigate root cause related to the impact of the ECS (EDNS0 Client Subnet) change
ERE-2018.longterm_mitigation: Investigate root cause related to the impact of the ECS (EDNS0 Client Subnet) change
ERE-2018.resolution_description: Rollback ECS (EDNS0 Client Subnet) change for ASN 20115
ERE-2018.root_cause:
The trigger for this incident was the ECS (EDNS0 Client Subnet) change pushed in EUM-291 .
The Nameserver space that remained unchanged on the last push of ECS suffered overlapping with the end-user BGPs that was added in certain cases. This generated that our system disregarded ECS from those Nameservers hence not using ECS and sending the traffic to other spaces ." and " Charter did not comply with the agreement of having their nameservers in a differentiated community space than their end-users. That overlap made our system dismiss those DNS to be used for ECS

- yospace respecting our ttls
- customer asked to put prinstine manifest on separate host and pristine workflow now on a more stable map - w99
- OS will also less contention wtih API traffic
ERE-2033.root_cause: Signs showed to be stale manifest being served through pristine workflow. The bulk of the reason that this looked to happen was due to Yospace not respecting our TTLs, combined with being on w16 (a map that changes often) and combined with Yospace will serve a stale manifest if older than 60.5s which we believe broke the player logic causing the 90% vpf.

10/12 - Discussed options with mapper, not many options available to overcome longer TTLs (example proxad in FR), best option we have is to keep FOM until AANP is willing to change TTL values. NS continues to escalate.
Blocker: AANP response

10/5 -  NSU
Blocker: no response from ISP over last week

9/27 -  Region is overloading due to mr96 hitting the max flits with being fully spread. This causes the whole region to hit 100% cpu util for ~3+ minutes. Region in question has been FOM capped to 70% and no longer hitting max flits. The cause seems to be associated to AANPs use of TTL being longer than expected (90s instead of 20s).  
Blocker - ISP/NS response
ERE-1671.root_cause:
AANP has TTLs that are longer than expected. We also see tiktok traffic ramping up on these regions, being mapped away, but traffic continues to go towards these regions. 
Fom capped for headroom until aanp can change manipulation of TTLs

ERE-1686.summary: {F-CS-7467799} - High SSL time seen for m.skybet.com
ERE-1686.root_cause: Customer had cname flattening(part of Cloudflare DNS service) causing serial and map not to be reported and in turn causing the load reporting to happen on base serial .Due to this even on high flits dnsp wasn't able to spread the load for more machines overwhelming them and causing slower SSL Handshakes.

ERE-1160.mitigation_plan: IAT need to work on metadata tweak with fixes
ERE-1160.resolution_description:
Advanced metadata adjusted with fixes. 

Since metrocache.data is evaluated after arl.data (metrocache.data has priority 12 in contexts.xml, while arl.data has priority 10)
The metrocache tier which is declared in metrocache-ff.xml and also called “MCH”, overrides the tier created by the client. Hence we got the following flow:
 
Clients TD0 becomes the Metrocache’s sureroute tier ->
It’s map (5209.mch-ff-lo-sr1388.akasrg.akamai.com) is not matched to any map declared in metrocache-id’s ->
We go to a TD0 which is actually a metrocache sro tier ->
We end up on a metrocache backend, without being admitted to metrocache.
ERE-1160.root_cause:
SR_MAP_SERIAL was not defined hence basic string exceptions 
MCH tier name was used which interfere with MCH product metadata for actual MCH tier.

ERE-1892.summary: Performance Degradation for CBS during NFL event
ERE-1892.mitigation_plan:
- Evaluate available public burst capacity in US and the additional headroom this could create.
- Use an alternate hostname/GTM property for ATT end users that could be mapped to a higher priority map ( could use dscw40 as the alternate map (for select ASNs). However, the priority for w40 will need to be increased ahead of the event). Needs Execs approval.
ERE-1892.longterm_mitigation: Increase PNI capacity with ATT in major metros where there’s currently a shortfall.
ERE-1892.executive_summary:
25/Jan
- On 14th-Jan, there were multiple events (primarily NFL online exclusive) on the platform which peaked at ~38T on the event map in US (with NBC alone pushing ~25T) and there was a drop in in-country mapping on w16 during the peak-hours.
- Capacity constraint within the US during the peak-window, mainly on AT&T on the event map, which is driving the TTLB higher.
- Shared available options with the IAT 
1. Increase PNI capacity with ATT in major metros where there’s currently a shortfall.
2. Evaluate available public burst capacity in US and the additional headroom this could create.
3. Use an alternate hostname/GTM property for ATT end users that could be mapped to a higher priority map ( could use dscw40 as the alternate map (for select ASNs). However, the priority for w40 will need to be increased ahead of the event). Needs Execs approval.


Performance degradation during NFL event causing reduced traffic share to Akamai. This was determined by increase TTLB for their test object between 2:30 - 3:30 AM UTC on Jan 14th. Investigation revealed high OOC mapping which appears to be due to increase traffic during that time.
ERE-1892.resolution_description:
- Evaluate available public burst capacity in US and the additional headroom this could create.

- Use an alternate hostname/GTM property for ATT end users that could be mapped to a higher priority map ( could use dscw40 as the alternate map (for select ASNs). However, the priority for w40 will need to be increased ahead of the event). Needs Execs approval.
ERE-1892.root_cause: Capacity constraint within the US during the peak-window, mainly on AT&T on the event map, which is driving the TTLB higher.

ERE-1303.summary: {F-CS-6922193} data shared between different customers when served from cache
ERE-1303.executive_summary:
From the given logs, Errors on cache_id 13976 (storeswapMetaFromRefiov()) should not cause wrong delivery it should set ERR_SWAPMETA_PARSE_FAIL for those reqs.
We have seen ether pad example of swap file no 4294967295 for many urls but this is essentially -1, it doesn't mean we are sharing same objects to other reqs.
Moreover, ERE team did spend considerable amount of time on this issue but we could not find much as the issue is not reproducible. We just observed some container file errors.
ERE-1303.root_cause: ERE team did spend considerable amount of time on this issue but we could not find much as the issue is not reproducible. We just observed some container file errors. Hence, we do not have any root  cause on why corrupted content was served.

few items to call out: 
1. We have issues being able to serve stale on bind, ff/essl will be upgraded to answerX
2. our ability to fail over in SDN will need to be improved

https://docs.google.com/document/d/1w-YFB9AMOVhouzh7N4fdDWCakH6aYasWFV5EpQ4gHgI/edit?userstoinvite=pat@plarkin.org&sharingaction=manageaccess&role=writer

ERE-1639, ERE-1636

8/23 - 
blocker: none 
RCA complete.  Few action items still remain on incident.
Brief summary up until this point.  Cogent transit had a down stream lossy issue which manifested as DNS errors for this ecor at large for this ecor, likely stemming from toplevel lookups which only would go through default transit, which was cogent here (sdn still chose cogent even if it was lossy and marked bad).

Lossy transit caused our ability to do DNS queries to fail

ERE-2466.executive_summary:
Nov 28:
A couple of issues have been identified and mitigated so far:
1: Coupangs' Traffic: A couple of their hostnames (with non-cacheble traffic) were using MCH unnecessarily. Since their traffic is spiky and the load was misreported, they were creating issues on Edge and TD0. As a mitigation, MCH was disabled (for non-cacheble) and load reporting was fixed.
2: Due to a (bad) PP switch, a portion of their clientbase (Alibaba DE) was mapping randomly between DE and US. With the help of mapops and nocc, the bad PP was redlisted, and earlier good PPs were assigned manually. Now mapping is fixed.

From the APIX cust dashboard, Client Aborts seem baselined now.
ERE-2466.root_cause:
Coupangs' traffic was interfering with TWC in the US, and due to a (bad) PP switch, a portion of their clientbase in Germany was impacted.
(ref. executive summary)

ERE-1896.summary: {F-CS-7996319} Connection refused errors from the Edge because of FF ghost rolls in APIX configuration since last Oct.
ERE-1896.executive_summary:
"Connection refused" in APIX / FF network but not in Ion / ESSL

Vivek from Ghost team suggested us to reach out to the Ghost SMEs (ghost-dev) for suggestion.
We did check the Swans logs and found that the mapper is not assigning any troubled ips to the end user. It Looks like customer's testing tool is DNS caching the ghost ip and trying to connect to the same ghost which is causing this issue.
ERE-1896.root_cause:
After checking the SWANs logs, we found that the the mapper is not assigning any troubled ips to the end user. Customer's testing tool is DNS caching the ghost ip and trying to connect to the same ghost which is causing this issue.
turning off the caching improved the result.
Ghost rolls in APIX core regions was due to underlying Ghost rolls due to QUIC Traffic.
Ghost team is already looking into it.

ERE-1228.root_cause: Customer telemetry data showed a 16% error rate on akamai during event, we had not much indication of an issue on our platform. ask was if a more expansive map would help or more prewarmed traffic - event on w16 which has plenty of capacity.  and prewarmed traffic, we had no signal if this needed to change.

ERE-1110.summary: {F-CS-6567233} - Nike traffic ERR_CONNECT_TIMEOUT from siteshield region
ERE-1110.mitigation_plan: The SS allowlist needs to be updated with more robust regions having headroom .

ERE-3039.summary: Recurring spiky traffic in r49882 causing client timeouts
ERE-3039.executive_summary: Customer reported 36 instances of timeout across 5 different timeframes when connecting to VIP 23.4.61.97. Reviewing the region, we see spiky traffic followed by self-suspensions around those times. We escalated to NOCC and the suggestion was to engage ghost SMEs for further investigation. The highest traffic during this time to that region was from Digicert hostnames. We're looking for options to prevent this issue from happening.
ERE-3039.root_cause: T-mobile cpcode 1625920 had flit spikes overwhelming machines Not directly impacting but the unassigned load on the region is high due to digicert's multiple hostname .

ERE-2848.summary: {F-CS-10129111} MSFT:  High 5xx Errors on assets.msn.cn/ntp.msn.cn due to SSL failures
ERE-2848.mitigation_plan: Moved to SNI slot 40480 and has been prespread.
ERE-2848.longterm_mitigation: Customer should follow best practices .  Other issues is concentrated traffic from fewer ASN (being origin to tencent) and spiky traffic needs to be handled better.
ERE-2848.executive_summary: - High 5xx Errors on assets.msn.cn/ntp.msn.cn due to SSL failures, related to new rule created in PM
ERE-2848.resolution_description: Moved to SNI slot 40480 and has been prespread.
ERE-2848.root_cause: Customer setup where Akamai is used as origin for Tencent and the objects are hardcoded to tencent-assets  . There is no DNS look up and spiky nature of the traffic caused machines/ regions to be overwhelmed causing ssl failures.
